messages.content: Make sure auto routing Doesnt draw NC or GND. And draws every other net as shortly as possible.

Done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `agents/autoroute/agent.py`

| Location | Change |
|---|---|
| After `routing_priority()` | Added `should_skip_net(net_name)` — returns `True` for **any GND variant** (`GND`, `AGND`, `PGND`, `DGND`, `GND_*`) and **any NC / no-connect net** |
| `build_initial_routing()` | Calls `should_skip_net()` at the top of the net loop — skipped nets are logged and never turned into traces |
| `get_routing_context()` | Calls `should_skip_net()` so GND/NC nets are **excluded from the context sent to the orchestrator** entirely |
| `check_all_nets_routed()` | Calls `should_skip_net()` so GND/NC nets are **never flagged as "unrouted"** in validation |

### `agents/autoroute/CLAUDE.md`

- Added explicit **"Nets to NEVER Route"** section (GND = copper pour, NC = leave floating)
- Added **"shortest path rule"** reminder to every priority tier: greedy MST + Manhattan segments, no detours

### Why GND is excluded
GND belongs on a **copper pour / ground plane**, not individual traces. Routing individual GND spiders degrades EMC, wastes copper, and is wrong practice. NC pads are intentionally floating by definition.
Area "copper pour" is not working properly. It should have a good clearance to all non nets.
